transcript: You are, uh, live here at, uh, Spectacle Theater.
Aw, yeah! Alright, outstanding! I’m happy to hear that. Well, uh, ya know, happy Saturday, I’d like to say.
So, uh, LPC, do you wanna take some, uh, questions?
Yes, please! Or we could make some calls, call some payphones—
M’kay! I got somethin’ here and it’s ringing.
Hi, yes, uh, I needed some help here tonight, please.
Oh, uh, yeah! You called yesterday, Percival.
Well, I’m working at a trade show.
So, I— I’m working at a trade show, so I can’t be on the phone. I’m very sorry. But you can feel free to message me, Percival.

Aw, shucks. They left. That’s okay! That’s okay. This gal, uh, apparently has gotten real hip real quick, is what she’s done. Okay, okay, I can dig it! So, uh… Let’s just move forward, shall we? Who’s with me on that? [sniffs] ‘Cause, ah—
Thank you. Thank you! Hey, sir, ma’am? Thank you.
Hey, Longmont, could I—? Could I ask you to make a call for me?
I was hoping you could call my mom.
Yeah, it’s, uh— Her phone number is [redacted]. Thank you.
We’ll get Nancy on the line, here. Alright. What town does she live in?
She’s in Rhode Island— In Barrington, Rhode Island.
Nancy? Oh, hi! Uh, yes, how ya doin’? [creaking]
I’m Chester. I’m, uh, your new neighbor. I got your number from the post office. And listen, I’m sorry to bother you. I’m new to the area, here. But they said—
What neighbor? Where are you my neighbor?
Oh, I’m just ‘round the corner, off to the right, there. My phone, uh, it actually doesn’t have—
Uh, I’m sorry, what town are you even talking about?
Oh, sure! Sure, I— It doesn’t have an “8”, so I needed to borrow your phone. I gotta make a very important phone call. If I pay you—?
What are you talking—? What are you talking about? Pardon me?—
I-if I maybe pay you some money? I need to make a very important phone call, uh-uh, Nancy. Here, tonight. And my phone—
—doesn’t have the numeral 8 on it, ya know, so— Wha—? [delay]
I-I-I know we’ve never met but I’d consider it a big, big favor!—
And I am not your neighbor and—
—I have no idea who you are.
Oh, yeah, we’ve met. I walked by when you were outside. Oh, sure. Oh! Uh-huh! [delay] Um, just for a few minutes—
And I’m not there now, anyway.
Well I can meet ya when it’s convenient for you. My phone does not have an 8; it does not have it. And I’ve gotta make a phone call—
I’m sorry. I-I’m sorry, you’re gonna have to get—
You’re gonna have to get, uh, your own phone.
No, I’ll just go to your door—
—and wait for you to come home and then when you get home, we’ll get on the phone!—
No, you will not, sir, or I’ll call the police.
The police? Well maybe they can—
You are not gonna wait at my door.
I’ll just wait in the backyard and then when you get home, we can look at—
No, you are not gonna wait— If—
If you are in my backyard when I get home, I will call the police.
I’m gonna— I’m gonna work— I’ll give ya $20, okay?
Lemme tell ya somethin’. Thomas’ mother, Nancy, is who was [sic] that was, right there! Uh-huh! Oh! Big time! Let’s move right along, shall we? Who’s with me up ‘ere? [exhales]
Wonderful! Thank you. And thank you for your patience. Uh, thank you for packing your packing your patience, as they say on the Traffic Report. We’re gonna get through this together as a team—
Good. Can you hold, please? I’m—
I don’t have a lotta time, here. [delay] I’m at a payphone. I just need to get a take out order, [delay] if I could. Is that okay?
It’s really comin’ down out here. It’s raining and—oh, god!—it’s thundering. I don’t know what to do—
Okay, what do you want, sir?
I-I gotta get some food! What’s your name? I didn’t catch your name.
[continual heavy rain sfx until call ends]
Whadda ya doin’ up ‘ere? [delay] Okay, do you have pie?
Do you have pie, for the second time? Hm?
’Kay, can I get an extra small pie?—
I can’t hear you; you keep echoing.
It’s raining! It’s like I said. I apologize, it’s pourin’ down here! I’m at a payphone!—
I— I apologize, too, ‘cause I’m— I’m just havin’ a hard time hearin’ ya—
Well I apologize again! Whadda ya think of that? I need an extra small pie—
Okay, I need an extra small apple pie, okay? And I need—
Extra small. Ya know, I’m tryin’ ta limit what I do, here—
I said— There’s— It comes in one size, sir.
I can’t be goin’ buckwild here, alright? Are you with me, or…? [stammers]
This lady expects me to go frickin’ buckwild with the pie! Ya know—
—and I try to tell her, “Look, I— Ya know, no! Not gonna happen!” Okay, I got another line ringin’ here. Let’s see what happens up here.
Just real quick—real quick—bear with me.
Thank you for calling the Doctor John’s in Midvale How may I assist you?
I’m good! How’re ya doin’ up ‘ere?! [delay] Uh, yeah, uh, what do you do here? [sniffs]
Um, it’s an adult store. Can I ask where you’re calling?
Right! I needed to get a couple publications. I was wondering if you have ‘em.
I’m not sure what that means.
Oh, magazines! Yeah! We do have a large selection of magazines.
Okay. Um. D’you have, uh, Pulp Grip at all?
Um, I’m not sure. We have so many different magazines. So you’ll have to come in and look. ‘Cause I’m— They’re all different types.
Um, how ‘bout Flipwad? Have you seen the June issue of Flipwad?
Oh, no, I don’t think we have any, like, recent monthly—monthly stuff. We just order shipment of, like, Hustler and Club.
Okay. Uh, how ‘bout Sea Heap, is the—the new one I was lookin’ for. It’s pretty darn new!
[laughs] I don’t know about any of that!—
We do have, like, [breaks up] and stuff—
Huh? O! [delay] You guys got, ah, buncha stuff up ‘ere I can grip ‘hold of tonight, or…? [exhales]
I’m lookin’ to grip ‘hold o’ some, ah, good stuff here, tonight. Ya know what I’m sayin’? [delay] Ya got the goods, or…?
[laughs] I’m not sure what you’re asking.
I need to grip ‘hold o’ somethin’ l—
—little later tonight, ya know what I’m sayin’?
Ah! [delay] Hey, are you—? What’re ya doin’ up ‘ere? [delay] How late you guys open, anyhow?
Aww! Okay, everybody’s gone. But, uh,
LPC, can you, uh, make a call?
Here, we’ve got a number for you.
Hi. Uh, it’s the— It’s the record store I used to—
Sound Garden. How can I help you?
Um, yeah, I needed to return a record. For a full refund.

Uh, I didn’t care for it! Was pretty inappropriate! [delay] From what I was, uh, expecting. I heard a washboard, I heard hand claps. It was supposed to be a gospel record. Inappropriate! So I need a cash refund on that, okay? Lookin’ for some assurances that when I get there, I can get my money back, ya understand? ‘Cause I live way far away, okay?
Um, pretty sure vinyl’s non-returnable. Pretty sure this is a prank call. Um, and yeah, there’s no—
A “prank call”? What does that mean—
—If there’s nothing wrong with the record itself…
Oh, there’s a lot wrong with it!
Hey! Uh, how late y’all open up ‘ere?—
Yeah, I’m goin’ to, um, start workin’ there tomorrow, is that okay? [delay] Um—
Okay. And how much can I expect to make—
No, actually, tomorrow’ll be el— It’ll be eleven tomorrow, ‘cause Sunday.
And so, monetarily, how much can I expect to make?
Workin’ there. I need to start work tomorrow. I need to make some money right away.
Okay, wha—? I mean, this call, man, dude. So, you’re startin’ to work up here—
Uh, we— [laughs] We haven’t hired anybody new, man. So, like I said, uh…
No, I’ll be there at 11. I’m just tryin’ to get a feel for it—you know, what I can expect up ‘ere. Ya know? [delay] So, uh… [delay]
Okay, that was Wax Trax and Sound Garden back-to-back.
They wised up and they hung up! I’m not gonna lie to ya, people of New York. Not gonna josh ya. Not gonna Joshua. Alright! I’m happy to try somethin’ else here.
Thanks for callin’ Nicolo’s. what can I help you with?
Hey, thank you! How ya doin?
Oh, ya can? I can barely hear ya up ‘ere. I’m at a payphone—
Yeah, it’s—it’s comin’ through a little weird, but I can hear ya.
Oh, you can? Uh, how many fingers am I holdin’ up? Or, wait. [delay]
Okay, how late—? No, okay, how—? [mocking laughter] [delay] So how late are you open tonight? Uh…
Uh, we’ll be open ’til midnight. We’re—
We stop serving full menu at 11:30.
M— I think ya serve later! So whadda ya think of that?
[mocking laughter] [delay] Okay, so, if I came in in about 20 minutes, can I get a pickup?
Oh, yeah? yeah-yeah-yeah-yeah! Yah-yah-yah-yah-yah? D’oh yah-yah! [delay] Can I place an order?
Okay, can I get a salad? [pitch-down] A dinner salad and then, uh, with, uh—
Large or small on the salad?—
[delay] Okay, can I get a salad? [quietly] A dinner salad and then, uh—
Large. Yeah, sure. And I need a buncha breadsticks, like 32 breadsticks.
[delay] [quietly] And I need a buncha breadsticks—like 24, uh, twenty—32 breadsticks—
And then, uh, as far as pizzas—
What kinda dressing did you want on that salad?
Thousand island, yeah. And then, uh—
As far as— Thousand island? It’s— It’s just like orange, ya know? I don’t really know how to describe—
We don’t have that for sure—
Oh. Yeahyeahyeah, yahyahyah. Uh, okay, no s— no dressing. I don’t know. So okay, um. Or if you, ya know, put somethin’ on the side, I can experiment around.
Ya know, you gotta experiment around, ya know? Yahyahyahyah! Sound good, or…?
I can— I can totally do that! Uh—
[delay] Somethin’ on the side, I can experiment around—
Wings. Wings. So, I need some wings. Wings, ya know?—
What kinda wings would ya like?—
Wings. Wings. [pitch-up] I need some wings. Yahyahyahyah!—
Uh, let’s see. I need wings. Ohyeahyeahyeah. Uh, buffalo, barbecue—
—barbecue? Kinda thing? Barbecue? And then I need about f—
[delay, pitch-up] Um, let’s see— I need wings. And I need about f—48, 60 o’ those.
[delay] Yeahyeahyeahyeah— [pitch-up, sped-up] 60 wings, uh, barbecue. Fountain drinks.
I can’t understand a word you’re sayin’. Your phone’s doin’ somethin’ really weird.
Really weird. I’m at a payphone. I’m on my way there. I’ll be there in 15 minutes. Um, fountain drinks?—
Yeah, I— I— We don’t do wings by number anyways; they’re by weight.
[delay] Really weird. I’m at a payphone. I’m on my way there. I’ll be there in 15 minutes. Um, fountain drinks?—
Since your order’s like a pound in bone-in wings—
10 Pounds. By pound, I can get about 10 lbs. of wings—
And then I need about, uh—
And then, uh, or f—10 lbs. of soda—
You sure you don’t want te—? You sure you don’t want ten liters?—
[delay] And then I need about, uh, 8 pounds of soda—
And then I need, uh, 6 pounds of, um, of salad—
—And, uh, ten pounds o’ pizza. Pepperoni pizza.
[delay, pitch-down] Uh, 6 pounds of, um, of salad—
Yeah, we’re def— We’re not doing anything by weight. I mean, uh, we’re not don’ 10 lbs. of fuckin’ pizza.
[delay] ten pounds o’ pizza. Pepperoni pizza—
[laughs] I tell you what, come in, order that here. I can—
Whadda ya mean? Whadda ya do—? Whadda ya mean?— [mocking laughter]
I can barely tell what you’re sayin’. Like, the phone’s really doin’ some weird shit.
…There. I’m at a payphone. I’m at my payphone here. I don’t— I—
Well, I— I can barely tell what you’re sayin’. You’re— It’s— Yeah. It’s doin’—
[delay, pitch-up, sped-up] Whadda ya mean? Whadda ya do—? Whadda ya mean?— [mocking laughter]
—It’s doin’, like, this weird repeating thing and it’s doin’ this weird thing like right now. It’s just—
You have reached the maximum time permitted for recording your message.
[delay, pitch-up, sped-up] Whadda ya mean? Whadda ya do—? Whadda ya mean?— [mocking laughter]
If you are satisfied with your message, press “1”—
[delay, pitch-up, sped-up] Whadda ya mean? Whadda ya do—? Whadda ya mean?— [mocking laughter]
To listen to your message, press “2”. To erase and re-record, press “3”—
Liter of— Uh, lemme just get a liter of salad and then I’ll be good to go, okay?—
[delay, pitch-up, sped-up] Whadda ya mean? Whadda ya do—? Whadda ya mean?— [mocking laughter]
[delay] Liter of— Uh, lemme just get a liter of salad and then I’ll be good to go, okay?—
Yeah, I can’t— I can’t— Like, now, there’s another voice [unclear]—
I need a liter of salad, end of s— end of sentence—
[delay] I need a liter of salad, end of s— end of sentence—
And I’ll be there within the next 10, 15 minutes, alright?
[delay] End o’ paragraph. And I’ll be there within the next 10, 15 minutes—
Um, I have no idea. I’m— I’m gonna have ta order to get here—
I can’t— I can’t tell what you’re sayin’.
What’s the freakin’ subtotal up there, Cap?
I got not total! I can’t— I can’t tell what you’re sayin’.
[delay] What’s the freakin’ subtotal up there, Cap?
Help me out! I just got outta the— I just got outta prison, ya know? This’s my— Been lookin’ forward to this.
[laughs] Yeah, come in. Make that order here.
Thank you. People of New York, thank you.
We’ve got one more phone number for ya.
Absolutely, absolutely! Here, okay, I’m ready.
It’s, uh, Jack Kublie; he just got a dog.
This— This is Sebastian. Who’s this?
That’s what I’m t— saying. Who is it? This is liquor store.
No, I need a liquor store, dude.
I need about 10 cases of Bud Light, but I’m in prison. Could you bring it up here, to the prison, if I pay extra?
No, we don’t deliver. Thanks, bye.
[laughs] God bless ya. We got time for one more, don’t we?
Yeah, yeah, this is— This is gonna be the last one.
I’ll straighten it up real frickin’ quick!—
[pre-call message of a Littleton, CO Domino’s plays]
Thanks for calling your Domino’s at Ken Caryl and Simms!—
This call may be monitored or recorded—
Be right with you to take your order!
Thank you for calling Domino’s. This is Cliff. How may I help you?
Hey, Cliff. I need to, uh, get six pizzas.
Okay. Hold on one second with me—
[reverb] Okay, sure, sure, I can hold on—
Can you just verify the phone number? Hello?—
Uh, 303, uh, [redacted]. It’s, uh, 303— It’s, uh, 303… Alright? And I need a large pepperoni with pineapple—
I’m sorry. Can you verify that number again? 303—?
[delay] Alright? And I need a large pepperoni with pineapple—
Uh, I— Sorry, I need that phone num— that phone number from ya—
Uh, I need a pineapple and I need a pepperoni pizza and I need a pineapple. Uh, I need a pineapple and I need a pepperoni—
Can I get—? Can I get—? Can I get your name?
—if I can get that from ya. My name’s Felipe [pitch-up] My name is Felipe. My name is Felipe, okay? My name’s Felipe Okay, did you catch that, or…?
Yeah, I did. Yes. Is this gonna be for carry-out or delivery?
[delay, pitch-down] Did you catch that? Okay—
I need this delivered. I’m gonna need this delivered, okay? Okay, yeah. Uh, I need—
[delay] I’m gonna need this delivered, okay?
Uh, 10274 West Chatfield Avenue. And my name’s Felipe.
And I need, uh, pepperoni, large, pineapple, large—
Sorry, your, um, something’s something’s echoing.
I can hear you just fine. Don’t worry about it—
And I need a 2-liter o’ Sprite.
[delay, pitch-up] I can hear you just fine. Don’t worry about it.
[delay, pitch-up] And I need a 2-liter o’ Sprite—
—about 8 liters of salad. I know you guys go in weight, not by quantity.
Hold on one— Hold one one second for me—
[delay] I know you guys go in weight, not by quantity—
I don’t have a lotta time; I’m at a payphone here, okay? So if I could get that and then I’d— So, I mean—
[delay] I don’t have a lotta time; I’m at a payphone here, okay? So if i could get that and then I’d—
If I can get some breadsticks—about 8 liters of breadsticks, okay?
Hey, I’m sorry. My— My guy’s havin’ a little bit of trouble hearin’ you—
[delay] If I can get some breadsticks—about 8 liters of breadsticks, okay?—
I say need ‘bout 8 liters o’ salad. He said he goes by weight, so I’d like to get about 8 liters of salad—uh, garden salad.
[delay]Oh! That’s okay. [laughs] I say need ‘bout 8 liters o’ salad. He said—
Okay? And then about, uh, 2 liters o’ Sprite—
Okay, I— Do you have like an echo goin’ on? I can’t really understand you very well—
Did you say, “8 liters of salad”?
Yes, I did. And you have a promotion, you said? What’s that about?—
Yeah, we— Our salads are pre-packaged; we don’t do ‘em by weight—
You got a promotion or somethin’? You got a special promotion up ‘ere?!
[delay] And you have a promotion, you said? What’s that about?
What’s the special? What’s on special?—
Oh, okay, I see what you’re sayin’. So about— How weigh—? How heavy is the salad, ya know? ‘Cause he said he likes—
—to go by weight, not by quantity. I know you guys are particular about that, so I wanna comply with ya there, okay?
Who are—? Who are you talking to? ‘Cause we don’t—
[delay] ’Cause he said he likes to go by weight, not by quantity—
[delay] I know you guys are particular about that, so I wanna comply with ya there, okay?
Okay. Okay, okay! That’s fine! Okay and then on the Sprite, how’re we don’ on the Sprite? ya got those poured yet? Uh, ya got those poured out?
[delay] Okay. Okay, okay! That’s fine! Okay and then on the Sprite, how’re we don’ on the Sprite?
Okay. [laughs] I love you guys! Thank you so much! You guys frickin’ rule!
Have a awesome night! Thank you! Thank you!
